title: Reported Speech
description: Report what people said using tense backshift, reporting verbs, and changes in time expressions.

activities.title: Reported speech rules and tense shifts

items.example: She said she was tired. / She told me she was tired.

items.front: Tense shift: present simple → past simple
items.back: 'I'm tired.' → She said she was tired.
items.example: 'I work here.' → He said he worked there.

items.front: Tense shift: present continuous → past continuous
items.back: 'I'm studying.' → She said she was studying.
items.example: 'They're waiting outside.' → He said they were waiting outside.

items.front: Tense shift: past simple → past perfect
items.back: 'I saw him.' → She said she had seen him.
items.example: 'We finished late.' → They said they had finished late.

items.front: Tense shift: will → would / can → could
items.back: 'I'll call you.' → He said he would call me.
items.example: 'I can help.' → She said she could help.

items.front: Reported questions (no inversion, no question mark)
items.back: 'Where do you live?' → She asked where I lived.
items.example: 'Are you coming?' → He asked if I was coming.

items.back: today→that day, now→then, tomorrow→the next day, yesterday→the day before
items.example: He said he'd call me the next day. (originally: 'I'll call you tomorrow.')

items.back: asked, told, explained, promised, warned, suggested, admitted
items.example: She warned me not to be late. He suggested going by train.

items.prompt: Direct: 'I'm really happy.' → Reported: She said she ___ really happy.
items.explanation: Present simple 'am' → past simple 'was' in reported speech.

items.prompt: Direct: 'I'll call you tomorrow.' → He said he ___ call me the next day.
Direct: 'Where do you work?' → She asked me where I ___.
items.explanation: Reported question: no inversion, past simple: she asked where I worked.

items.prompt: Direct: 'Don't be late!' → He ___ me not to be late.
items.explanation: 'Warned' is the most appropriate reporting verb for a strong warning not to do something.

items.prompt: Direct: 'I have finished the report.' → She said she ___ the report.
items.explanation: Present perfect 'have finished' → past perfect 'had finished' in reported speech.

items.sentence: Direct: 'I'm hungry.' → She said she ___ hungry.
Direct: 'We can meet on Friday.' → They said they ___ meet on Friday.
Direct: 'Are you coming?' → He asked ___ I was coming.
Direct: 'I saw him yesterday.' → She said she ___ him the day before.
Direct: 'Don't touch that!' → He ___ us not to touch it.
